lecturer_role: guest lecturer; Prof. Eagar present and occasionally interjecting
topic_primary: Weld inspection and Certified Welding Inspectors
topics: Definition of quality in manufactured products
Welding codes, specifications, recommended practices, guides
Role and duties of the Certified Welding Inspector (CWI)
CWI certification requirements (education, experience, exam, vision)
CWI personal attributes (KASH: Knowledge, Attitude, Skills, Habits)
Joint types (butt, corner, T, lap, edge)
Welding symbols (reference line, arrow, tail, weld symbol vs. welding symbol)
Electrode identification systems (SMAW, GMAW, FCAW, SAW)
Structure of the AWS D1.1 code
cases_referenced: Mike Balfour's prior National Lab work on nuclear weapon components (every-part inspection example)
notes: Guest lecture by Mike Balfour. Tom Eagar audibly interjects briefly (inaudible on auto-captions) during the welding symbols discussion around 28:00–29:00. The captioner produces unusually heavy duplicate-line artifacts in this transcript; many cleanup decisions were duplicate-line removal rather than substantive editorial choices. Several technical proper-noun captioner errors (porosity→paracity, fillet→Philip, E7018→e718, flux-cored→flux cord) corrected silently. One probable factual slip by Mike (D1.3 as aluminum code; canonically D1.2) preserved per convention §16 and flagged for the editorial register.
